supervisor.reason: The CLI help and docs clarification slice is implemented and validated: top-level help now exposes runtime versus daemon config entry points, and README/GUIDE explain how dormammu.json, DORMAMMU_CONFIG_PATH, and daemonize.json fit together.

bootstrap.goal: Clarify the Dormammu CLI so operators can discover daemonize and understand JSON config injection from help text and docs.

tasks.resume_checkpoint: Resume after the CLI help and documentation clarification slice. The next step is optional commit preparation or any follow-up UX polish around typo recovery or command aliases.

items.text: Phase 1. Inspect the current CLI help text, command surface, and config loading behavior to identify why `daemonize` and JSON config entry points are hard to discover
Phase 2. Redesign the top-level and subcommand help text so general runtime config and daemon JSON config usage are explicit in `--help`
Phase 3. Update `README.md` with a clearer command chooser, `show-config` usage, and side-by-side examples for `dormammu.json` and `daemonize.json`
Phase 4. Update `docs/GUIDE.md` so operators can quickly distinguish runtime config resolution from daemon queue config injection
Phase 5. Add focused CLI help regression tests and run targeted validation for the new help and documentation surface

next_action: Prepare a commit for the CLI help and documentation clarification slice, or consider a small UX follow-up such as typo-tolerant aliases for daemonize.
notes: Treat this file as machine truth and keep Markdown synchronized.
DASHBOARD.md should show the actual progress of the active scope.
PLAN.md should list prompt-derived development items in `[ ] Phase N. <title>` format.
The installed guidance bundle and packaged asset copy should stay synchronized.
Legacy TASKS.md snapshots should be migrated forward into PLAN.md without losing checklist state.
A new prompt-driven scope now targets daemonized prompt watching from JSON configuration.
The repository does not currently contain .dev/PROJECT.md or .dev/ROADMAP.md, so planning is grounded in the direct request, AGENTS.md, current code, and machine state.
Daemon mode needs inotify-first monitoring with a 60-second polling fallback where inode-backed watching is unavailable.
The feature request requires separating dormammu workflow skills from per-phase external agent CLI command configuration.
The daemonize design is captured in docs/daemonize-design.md and has been implemented in the new backend/dormammu/daemon package.
A follow-up request asked for clearer command discovery and explicit JSON config injection guidance in help text and docs.
Focused validation passed through python3 -m unittest tests.test_cli.
